introduction: This spec defines the team management subsystem for af-hub, implementing lightweight team CRUD and membership endpoints (admin only) built on top of the server foundation (spec 1). It establishes the `teams` and `team_members` database schema and enforces team lifecycle, slug/name/URL validation, and uniqueness constraints as the foundation for future RBAC work.

glossary.DELETE /api/v1/teams/:id: The HTTP endpoint for deleting an archived team.
glossary.GET /api/v1/teams: The HTTP endpoint for listing teams.
glossary.GET /api/v1/teams/:id: The HTTP endpoint for fetching a single team by ID.
glossary.GET /api/v1/teams/:id/members: The HTTP endpoint for listing members of a team.
glossary.POST /api/v1/teams: The HTTP endpoint for creating a new team.
glossary.POST /api/v1/teams/:id/archive: The HTTP endpoint for archiving an active team.
glossary.POST /api/v1/teams/:id/members: The HTTP endpoint for adding a user to a team.
glossary.POST /api/v1/teams/:id/reactivate: The HTTP endpoint for reactivating an archived team.
glossary.active: A team lifecycle state in which the team is operational and members can be added.
glossary.admin middleware: Middleware provided by server_foundation (spec 1) that verifies the authenticated caller is an admin, returning HTTP 403 otherwise.
glossary.archived: A team lifecycle state in which the team is inactive; members cannot be added, but the team is still readable.
glossary.auth middleware: Middleware provided by server_foundation (spec 1) that authenticates the caller from the request.
glossary.created_at: A UTC RFC3339 microsecond-precision timestamp column present on both `teams` and `team_members` tables recording when the row was inserted.
glossary.deleted: A terminal team lifecycle state in which the team record has been physically removed from the database; treated as non-existent by all endpoints.
glossary.embed.FS: A Go standard-library mechanism for embedding static files (such as SQL migration files) into the compiled binary.
glossary.error envelope: The standard JSON error response shape `{"code": <int>, "message": <string>}` defined by server_foundation and reused by all team endpoints.
glossary.include_archived: An optional boolean query parameter on the list-teams endpoint that, when `true`, causes archived teams to be included in the response alongside active teams.
glossary.joined_at: The original timestamp when a user membership was created, sourced from `team_members.created_at`; never updated on idempotent re-adds.
glossary.migration runner: Infrastructure provided by server_foundation (spec 1) that discovers and applies numbered SQL migration files in ascending order.
glossary.partial UNIQUE index: A SQLite index enforcing uniqueness only among rows satisfying a WHERE clause (e.g., `WHERE status != 'deleted'`), used to allow reuse of deleted teams' names and slugs.
glossary.slug: A URL-friendly identifier for a team: lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens, 3–64 characters, starting with a letter, not ending with a hyphen.
glossary.status: The lifecycle state of a team; one of `active`, `archived`, or `deleted`.
glossary.team_members: The database table that stores team membership records, owned by this spec.
glossary.teams: The database table that stores team records, owned by this spec.
glossary.updated_at: A UTC RFC3339 microsecond-precision timestamp column on the `teams` table that is updated whenever the team record is mutated (e.g., on archive or reactivate).
glossary.user_id: The UUID primary key of a user in the `users` table, used to identify membership.
glossary.users: The database table owned by the oauth_and_users spec that provides user identity data (id, email, name) joined for member responses.
glossary.\"invalid id format\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"invalid request body\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"invalid slug format\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"invalid team name\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"invalid url format\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"missing required field\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"team is already active\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"team is already archived\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"team is archived\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"team must be archived before deletion\: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"team name already exists\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"team not found\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"team slug already exists\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.\"user not found\": Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary./api/v1/teams: API endpoint path or route segment.
glossary.2026-07-10T15:01:11.889182Z: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.:id: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.?include_archived=true: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.Content-Type: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.Content-Type: application/json: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.

glossary.email: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.
glossary.id: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.

glossary.team_id: Code reference or literal value used in requirements.

requirements.title: Database schema migrations for teams and team_members

user_story.goal: have the `teams` and `team_members` tables created by numbered SQL migration files embedded via `embed.FS`
user_story.benefit: the database schema is version-controlled and applied deterministically by the `migration runner`

acceptance_criteria.action: creates the `teams` table with columns: `id` (TEXT PK, UUID), `name` (TEXT), `slug` (TEXT), `url` (TEXT nullable), `status` (TEXT, one of `active`/`archived`/`deleted`), `created_at` (DATETIME UTC), `updated_at` (DATETIME UTC); and applies a partial UNIQUE index on `name` WHERE `status` != 'deleted' and a partial UNIQUE index on `slug` WHERE `status` != 'deleted'

creates the `team_members` table with columns: `team_id` (TEXT FK referencing `teams`.id with cascade delete), `user_id` (TEXT FK referencing `users`.id), `created_at` (DATETIME UTC); composite primary key (`team_id`, `user_id`)

are embedded Go files using `embed.FS`, assigned sequential numeric prefixes at integration time to avoid conflicts with other specs' migrations, and discovered and applied in ascending numeric order by the `migration runner`

edge_cases.action: skips re-applying it, ensuring each migration is executed exactly once
edge_cases.error_condition: a migration file has already been applied

user_story.goal: create a new team by posting name, slug, and optional URL
user_story.benefit: teams can be established as organizational groupings before permissions are layered on

acceptance_criteria.action: trims leading/trailing whitespace from `name`, validates `name` (non-empty after trim, ≤255 chars), validates `slug` format (regex `^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$`), validates `url` format (http/https scheme and host, if provided), checks uniqueness of `name` and `slug` among non-deleted teams, inserts the new team with `status` = `active` and current UTC timestamps for `created_at` and `updated_at` within a transaction
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 201 with team object JSON: {id, name, slug, url, status, created_at, updated_at}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin sends a valid POST request with `name`, `slug`, and optional `url`

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 422 and error envelope message `"invalid team name"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the trimmed `name` is empty or exceeds 255 characters

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 422 and error envelope message `"invalid slug format"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `slug` does not match the required format

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 422 and error envelope message `"invalid url format"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `url` is provided but does not have an http or https scheme and a host

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 409 and error envelope message `"team name already exists"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a non-deleted team with the same `name` already exists

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 409 and error envelope message `"team slug already exists"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a non-deleted team with the same `slug` already exists

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope message `"invalid request body"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request body is malformed JSON or cannot be decoded

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 422 with error envelope message `"missing required field"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a required field (`name` or `slug`) is absent from the request body

acceptance_criteria.action: silently ignores unrecognized body fields (lenient parsing)
maps the DB constraint error to HTTP 409 with the appropriate message (`"team name already exists"` or `"team slug already exists"`)
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 409, {"code": 409, "message": "team name already exists"} or {"code": 409, "message": "team slug already exists"}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: a concurrent insert causes a database-level `partial UNIQUE index` violation on `name` or `slug` after the application-layer check passes

edge_cases.action: performs the application-layer uniqueness check and insert within a single database transaction, so that a race between two concurrent creates with the same `name` or `slug` is caught either by the application-layer check or the `partial UNIQUE index` constraint, never producing duplicate rows
allows reuse of `name` or `slug` values that previously belonged to a `deleted` team, because uniqueness is scoped to non-deleted teams only

user_story.goal: retrieve all active (or active + archived) teams
user_story.benefit: I can see the current organizational groupings and their statuses

acceptance_criteria.action: queries the `teams` table for all rows with `status` = `active`, ordered by `created_at` ascending, and returns them
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with JSON array of team objects (may be empty)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin sends a GET request without `include_archived`

acceptance_criteria.action: queries the `teams` table for all rows with `status` in (`active`, `archived`), ordered by `created_at` ascending, and returns them
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with JSON array of team objects (may be empty)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin sends a GET request with `?include_archived=true`

acceptance_criteria.action: never includes teams with `status` = `deleted` in any response, regardless of query parameters
returns all matching records with no pagination limit, ordered by `created_at` ascending

user_story.goal: fetch a single team by its UUID
user_story.benefit: I can inspect the full details of any active or archived team

acceptance_criteria.action: retrieves the team record from the `teams` table and returns it
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin requests a team by a valid UUID that belongs to an `active` or `archived` team

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 with error envelope message `"team not found"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the UUID does not match any team or matches a `deleted` team

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope message `"invalid id format"` before performing any database lookup
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:id` path parameter is not a valid UUID

user_story.benefit: inactive teams are hidden from default listings while preserving their data

acceptance_criteria.action: updates the team's `status` to `archived` and sets `updated_at` to the current UTC timestamp
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with updated team object JSON
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin posts to the archive endpoint for a team with `status` = `active`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 409 with error envelope message `"team is already archived"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team already has `status` = `archived`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 with error envelope message `"team not found"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team does not exist or has `status` = `deleted`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope message `"invalid id format"` before any database lookup
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:id` path parameter is not a valid UUID

acceptance_criteria.action: ignores any request body and does not enforce `Content-Type`

user_story.benefit: previously archived teams can be brought back into active use

acceptance_criteria.action: updates the team's `status` to `active` and sets `updated_at` to the current UTC timestamp
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with updated team object JSON
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin posts to the reactivate endpoint for a team with `status` = `archived`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 409 with error envelope message `"team is already active"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team already has `status` = `active`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 with error envelope message `"team not found"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team does not exist or has `status` = `deleted`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope message `"invalid id format"` before any database lookup
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:id` path parameter is not a valid UUID

acceptance_criteria.action: ignores any request body and does not enforce `Content-Type`

user_story.goal: permanently delete an archived team and all its memberships
user_story.benefit: teams that are no longer needed can be fully removed, releasing their name and slug for reuse

acceptance_criteria.action: within a single database transaction, deletes all rows in `team_members` with matching `team_id`, then deletes the team row from `teams`; if any step fails, the transaction is rolled back
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin sends a DELETE request for a team with `status` = `archived`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 409 with error envelope message `"team must be archived before deletion"`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 409, {"code": 409, "message": "team must be archived before deletion"}
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team has `status` = `active`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 with error envelope message `"team not found"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team does not exist or has already been `deleted`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope message `"invalid id format"` before any database lookup
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:id` path parameter is not a valid UUID

edge_cases.action: rolls back the entire transaction so neither the team record nor its memberships are removed, and returns an HTTP 500 error
edge_cases.error_condition: the database transaction partially fails (e.g., `team_members` deletion succeeds but `teams` row deletion fails)

user_story.goal: add a user to a team by posting their user_id
user_story.benefit: team membership is established and the current user profile is immediately reflected in the response

acceptance_criteria.action: within a transaction, inserts a row into `team_members` with `team_id`, `user_id`, and `created_at` = current UTC timestamp; then performs a fresh JOIN against `users` to retrieve `email` and `name`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with member object JSON: {user_id, team_id, email, name, joined_at}
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin posts a valid `user_id` for a user not yet a member of the team and the team has `status` = `active`

acceptance_criteria.action: performs a no-op (does not update `team_members`), then performs a fresh JOIN against `users` to retrieve current `email` and `name`; `joined_at` reflects the original membership `created_at`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with member object JSON reflecting current user data and original `joined_at`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin posts a `user_id` for a user who is already a member of the team

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 409 with error envelope message `"team is archived"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team has `status` = `archived`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 with error envelope message `"team not found"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team does not exist or has `status` = `deleted`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 with error envelope message `"user not found"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `user_id` does not correspond to any existing user in the `users` table

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope message `"invalid id format"` before any database lookup
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:id` path parameter or the `user_id` body field is not a valid UUID

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope message `"invalid request body"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the request body is malformed JSON or cannot be decoded

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 422 with error envelope message `"missing required field"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: `user_id` is absent from the request body

edge_cases.action: performs the membership existence check and insert (or no-op) within a single database transaction to prevent race conditions between two concurrent requests adding the same member; the composite primary key (`team_id`, `user_id`) on `team_members` provides the database-level guard

user_story.goal: list all members of a team
user_story.benefit: I can see who belongs to a team, including their current email and name

acceptance_criteria.action: queries `team_members` joined with `users` for rows matching the team ID, ordered by `team_members`.`created_at` ascending, and returns all records
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 with JSON array of member objects (may be empty)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an admin requests members for a valid team that is `active` or `archived`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 404 with error envelope message `"team not found"`
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the team does not exist or has `status` = `deleted`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope message `"invalid id format"` before any database lookup
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the `:id` path parameter is not a valid UUID

acceptance_criteria.action: returns all records with no pagination, ordered by `joined_at` ascending (oldest member first)

requirements.title: Admin middleware enforcement on all team routes

user_story.goal: receive a clear rejection when accessing team endpoints
user_story.benefit: team management is restricted to admins only

acceptance_criteria.action: applies the `admin middleware` from server_foundation to all routes under `/api/v1/teams` before any handler logic executes
returns HTTP 403 as defined by the `admin middleware` in server_foundation, before reaching any team handler
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 with error envelope defined by server_foundation
acceptance_criteria.error_condition: the authenticated user is not an admin

user_story.goal: have lifecycle transitions enforced so teams can only move through valid states
user_story.benefit: data integrity is maintained and invalid states are prevented

acceptance_criteria.action: permits only the transition to `archived`; any other lifecycle mutation (reactivate) returns HTTP 409
permits transition to `active` (reactivate) or physical deletion; any other lifecycle mutation is rejected
all lookup and mutation attempts return HTTP 404 because the record no longer exists; no recovery is possible
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 404 for any request targeting a deleted team's ID

acceptance_criteria.action: updates `updated_at` to the current UTC timestamp on every successful lifecycle transition (archive, reactivate); does not update `updated_at` on deletion since the record is removed

requirements.title: Uniform response format and content type

user_story.goal: receive consistently structured JSON responses with correct content type headers
user_story.benefit: client code can reliably parse responses without special-casing

acceptance_criteria.action: set `Content-Type: application/json` on all successful JSON responses
format all error responses using the `error envelope` structure `{"code": <int>, "message": <string>}` where `code` mirrors the HTTP status code
format all timestamps in `teams` and `team_members` responses as UTC RFC3339 with microsecond precision (e.g., `2026-07-10T15:01:11.889182Z`)

correctness_properties.title: Deleted team IDs are inaccessible across all endpoints
correctness_properties.for_any: team that has been physically deleted from the `teams` table
correctness_properties.invariant: every endpoint returns HTTP 404 for that team's ID, and no endpoint returns any data for it

correctness_properties.title: Name and slug uniqueness scoped to non-deleted teams
correctness_properties.for_any: pair of non-deleted teams in the `teams` table
correctness_properties.invariant: no two non-deleted teams share the same `name` or the same `slug`; this is enforced at both the application layer and via `partial UNIQUE index` constraints at the database layer

correctness_properties.title: Team lifecycle transitions are strictly ordered
correctness_properties.invariant: the `status` field only transitions from `active` → `archived` → `deleted`; the `active` → `deleted` direct transition is always rejected with HTTP 409

correctness_properties.title: Delete cascade is atomic
correctness_properties.invariant: either both the team row in `teams` and all its rows in `team_members` are removed, or neither is removed; partial deletion never occurs

correctness_properties.title: Member add is idempotent with fresh user data
correctness_properties.for_any: add-member request for a (team_id, user_id) pair that already exists in `team_members`
correctness_properties.invariant: the `team_members` row is not duplicated or mutated; the response always reflects current `email` and `name` from `users` via a fresh JOIN, and `joined_at` always equals the original `team_members`.`created_at`

correctness_properties.title: List ordering is stable and complete
correctness_properties.for_any: list response from GET /api/v1/teams or GET /api/v1/teams/:id/members
correctness_properties.invariant: results are ordered by `created_at` ascending (for teams) or `joined_at` ascending (for members), contain all matching records with no pagination omissions, and never include `deleted` teams

correctness_properties.title: updated_at reflects every team mutation
correctness_properties.for_any: any archive or reactivate operation on a team
correctness_properties.invariant: `updated_at` is set to the current UTC timestamp on successful completion; it is not modified by membership changes and is not observable after deletion

execution_paths.title: Admin creates a new team successfully

steps.action: sends POST /api/v1/teams with valid name, slug, and optional url in JSON body
authenticates the caller from the request token
verifies the caller is an admin; proceeds if so, otherwise returns HTTP 403
parses the request body; trims name; validates name, slug format, and url format
opens a database transaction and queries `teams` for any non-deleted team with the same name or slug
inserts a new row into `teams` with status = `active` and current UTC timestamps; commits the transaction
returns HTTP 201 with the team object JSON to the admin

execution_paths.title: Admin archives then deletes a team with cascade

steps.action: sends POST /api/v1/teams/:id/archive for an active team
verifies the caller is an admin
validates the UUID path parameter; fetches the team from `teams`; confirms status is `active`; updates status to `archived` and sets `updated_at`
returns HTTP 200 with updated team object to the admin
sends DELETE /api/v1/teams/:id for the now-archived team
verifies the caller is an admin
validates the UUID; fetches the team; confirms status is `archived`; opens a transaction
deletes all rows in `team_members` with matching team_id, then deletes the team row; commits the transaction
returns HTTP 204 to the admin

execution_paths.title: Admin adds a member to an active team (idempotent)

steps.action: sends POST /api/v1/teams/:id/members with a valid user_id JSON body
verifies the caller is an admin
validates UUID path parameter and user_id; fetches team from `teams`; confirms status is `active`; verifies user exists in `users`
opens a transaction; checks if (team_id, user_id) row already exists in `team_members`; inserts if new, no-ops if existing; commits
performs a fresh JOIN of `team_members` and `users` to assemble the member object with current email, name, and original joined_at
returns HTTP 200 with member object JSON to the admin

execution_paths.title: Concurrent duplicate team creation race resolved by partial UNIQUE index

steps.action: sends POST /api/v1/teams with slug 'my-team'; handler passes application-layer uniqueness check
concurrently sends POST /api/v1/teams with slug 'my-team'; handler also passes application-layer uniqueness check before Admin A's transaction commits
commits the insert successfully; returns HTTP 201
encounters a `partial UNIQUE index` violation on `slug` at the database layer; maps the error to HTTP 409 with message "team slug already exists"
returns HTTP 409 to Admin B, ensuring no duplicate non-deleted teams share the same slug

execution_paths.title: Full end-to-end admin team management flow from auth through database

steps.action: sends POST /api/v1/teams with name, slug, url over HTTPS
validates the bearer token and identifies the caller
checks the caller's role from the `users` table; proceeds because the caller is an admin
validates input fields; opens a transaction against the SQLite database via the DB connection from server_foundation; checks uniqueness; inserts into `teams`; commits
returns HTTP 201 with team object; admin records the new team's UUID
sends POST /api/v1/teams/:id/members with user_id to add a user
verifies admin role
verifies team exists and is `active`; verifies user exists in `users`; inserts membership row in `team_members` within a transaction; JOINs `users` for fresh member data
returns HTTP 200 with member object including email and name from `users` and original `joined_at`
sends GET /api/v1/teams/:id/members to confirm membership
queries `team_members` JOIN `users` ordered by joined_at ascending; returns HTTP 200 with array containing the newly added member

error_handling.condition: Duplicate team name among non-deleted teams (application-layer check)
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 409 with error envelope {"code": 409, "message": "team name already exists"}

error_handling.condition: Duplicate team slug among non-deleted teams (application-layer check)
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 409 with error envelope {"code": 409, "message": "team slug already exists"}

error_handling.condition: Database partial UNIQUE index violation on name or slug during concurrent insert
error_handling.behavior: Maps the DB constraint error to HTTP 409 with the appropriate message ("team name already exists" or "team slug already exists")

error_handling.condition: Invalid slug format (does not match regex ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]{1,62}[a-z0-9]$)
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 422 with error envelope {"code": 422, "message": "invalid slug format"}

error_handling.condition: Invalid URL format (missing http/https scheme or host)
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 422 with error envelope {"code": 422, "message": "invalid url format"}

error_handling.condition: Team name is empty after trimming or exceeds 255 characters
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 422 with error envelope {"code": 422, "message": "invalid team name"}

error_handling.condition: Required field (name, slug, or user_id) is missing from the request body
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 422 with error envelope {"code": 422, "message": "missing required field"}

error_handling.condition: Request body is malformed JSON or cannot be decoded on an endpoint that requires a body
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 400 with error envelope {"code": 400, "message": "invalid request body"}

error_handling.condition: Path parameter :id or user_id is not a valid UUID
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 400 with error envelope {"code": 400, "message": "invalid id format"} before any database lookup

error_handling.condition: Team does not exist or has status = deleted
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 404 with error envelope {"code": 404, "message": "team not found"}

error_handling.condition: Archive attempted on a team that is already archived
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 409 with error envelope {"code": 409, "message": "team is already archived"}

error_handling.condition: Reactivate attempted on a team that is already active
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 409 with error envelope {"code": 409, "message": "team is already active"}

error_handling.condition: Delete attempted on a team with status = active
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 409 with error envelope {"code": 409, "message": "team must be archived before deletion"}

error_handling.condition: Delete transaction partially fails (membership deletion succeeds but team row deletion fails)
error_handling.behavior: Rolls back the entire transaction; returns HTTP 500 with error envelope

error_handling.condition: Add member attempted on a team with status = archived
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 409 with error envelope {"code": 409, "message": "team is archived"}

error_handling.condition: User not found when adding a member
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 404 with error envelope {"code": 404, "message": "user not found"}

error_handling.condition: Caller is not an admin (verified by admin middleware)
error_handling.behavior: Returns HTTP 403 as defined by the admin middleware in server_foundation, before any handler logic executes
